violations.text: Several foods (see disposal form) were observed held at 45-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
A refrigerator was in poor repair at time of inspection (not holding adequate ambient temperature). New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
Cheese (not observed on exemption list) was observed held past its seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The establishment was observed reduced oxygen packaging raw and cooked seafood. The establishment is not permitted to ROP any food items at this time. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The presence of ants is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of live and dead ants observed at time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2023
